description: A slope chart: a measure plotted against a truncated-quarter date, with a template-internal FIRST/LAST filter keeping only the two endpoint periods; one line per category shows the direction/magnitude of change between the two periods.

slots.notes: one slope line per member; also the line-end label. Template column carries aggregate-role-from='[State/Province]' — see hazard hidden-aggregate-role-from.
the measure whose endpoint values define each slope; also the value label.
truncated-quarter date on the columns axis; the FIRST/LAST filter reduces it to the two endpoint periods.

hazards.detail: A template-internal boolean table calc 'FIRST()==0 or LAST()==0' filters to the two endpoint periods. It is NON-self-closing (nested <table-calc>), so parseTemplateCalcs (self-closing only) does NOT see it — it is NOT a declared calc slot and rides along verbatim. It has NO bindable inputs (FIRST/LAST are positional), so it is portable, but it assumes the bound temporal spans ≥2 periods; a single-period bind degenerates to one endpoint.
[Region] aggregate-role-from='[State/Province]' is a cosmetic secondary-role hint that is not rewritten onto the bound field (same behavior as correlation-scatter-plot-chart).
